commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=0f24d9e843539114517409382af55e2a8b78d7c0)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds source-tagged Codex app-server commentary progress dedupe across typed/raw lanes plus regression tests for typed-first, raw-first, and raw-first chunked mirror cases.

PR surface: Source +21, Tests +145. Total +166 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-level reproduction is high confidence: current main emits commentary from both typed and raw Codex app-server lanes with different ids, and downstream dispatch flushes commentary when the item id changes. I did not run live Discord in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] A maintainer should require a patch update, captured real behavior proof, and canonical branch selection; ClawSweeper should not queue an automated repair while contributor proof remains missing.

target.title: Duplicate 💬 commentary notes with openai/gpt-5.4-mini (codex app-server) in verbose mode
target.body:
## Summary
- Fix classification: Root-cause fix.
- Maintainer-ready confidence: High.
- Root cause: The duplicate `💬` commentary note came from a projector state invariant violation: Codex app-server emits the same assistant commentary text through two upstream notification lanes, typed `agentMessage` events and raw `rawResponseItem/completed` events, but OpenClaw keyed commentary progress identity only by the lane-specific item id; because the typed and raw lanes use different ids for the same commentary note, downstream dispatch received two independent preamble items and flushed both as separate Discord progress messages.
- Why this is root-cause fix: The fix restores the projector-level commentary identity invariant before channel delivery by deduping adjacent cross-lane mirrors with different item ids and equivalent normalized text, including the raw-first lifecycle where the raw lane has the complete text and typed `agentMessage` later arrives as chunked deltas; this addresses the source event projection boundary that caused the duplicate, instead of adding a Discord-only suppression workaround after the duplicate item events already exist.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/event-projector.test.ts`; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts -t commentary`.
- Observed result after fix: Projector regression tests passed for typed-first, raw-first, and raw-first chunked typed-delta lane mirrors, and downstream commentary dispatch validation passed.
- What did NOT change: Only Codex app-server commentary progress projection is changed; final-answer resolution, non-commentary assistant streaming, raw assistant text collection, same-item commentary progress updates, and channel dispatch buffering are unchanged.
- What problem does this PR solve? It prevents duplicate Discord `💬` commentary progress messages for one Codex app-server verbose-mode commentary note.
- What is the intended outcome? A single commentary note mirrored by Codex typed and raw lanes becomes one OpenClaw progress event.
- What should reviewers focus on? The cross-lane dedupe conditions in the Codex event projector and the raw-first chunked delta regression coverage.

## Linked context
- Fixes #93296.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)
- Behavior or issue addressed: Duplicate `💬` commentary notes from Codex app-server verbose-mode typed/raw lane mirrors.
- Real environment tested: OpenClaw Codex app-server projector and auto-reply commentary dispatch unit tests, including extension-specific validation for `extensions/codex`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/event-projector.test.ts`
- Exit code: 0
- Evidence after fix: Copied terminal output from the projector validation run:
```text
RUN  v4.1.7 [redacted-local-path]

Test Files  1 passed (1)
     Tests  93 passed (93)
```
- Observed result after fix: Projector coverage passes for commentary progress streaming plus typed-first, raw-first, and raw-first chunked typed-delta dedupe cases.
- What was not tested: Live Discord delivery was not available in this environment, so no live Discord message was sent; local validation completed with the projector and downstream dispatch tests passing.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: Validation is unit-level coverage of the projector and downstream dispatch behavior using the existing Vitest harness.

## Tests and validation
- Extension-specific validation: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/event-projector.test.ts`
  ```text
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
       Tests  93 passed (93)
  ```
- Downstream commentary dispatch validation: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts -t commentary`
  ```text
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
       Tests  6 passed | 203 skipped (209)
  ```
- Regression coverage: typed-first mirror dedupe, raw-first mirror dedupe, raw-first with chunked typed deltas, existing commentary streaming, and downstream commentary dispatch buffering.

## Risk checklist
- Highest risk area: Over-deduping legitimate commentary progress updates.
- Mitigation: The dedupe is limited to adjacent cross-lane mirrors with different item ids, while same-lane same-item progress updates still use the existing per-item snapshot behavior. The existing commentary streaming test still expects incremental same-item updates.

## Current review state
- Local Codex diff review completed without findings after the chunked raw-first case was added.
